sources.notes: Statement and constraints: maintain an initially empty array under rotate-last-to-front, reverse, and append operations, outputting sum a_i*i after every operation; total q <= 2e5.
Official editorial source for Codeforces Round 1017; used to confirm the intended O(1) maintenance of the weighted sum under the three operations.
Luogu solution derives the O(1) formulas for cyclic shift, reverse, and append using ans, size, and total sum.
Independent Luogu solution cross-checks the deque plus reverse-flag implementation and the formula ans = (n+1)*sum - ans after reversal.

annotation.summary: Maintain a dynamic array under three persistent operations and output the weighted index sum sum a_i*i after each operation.
annotation.constraints: The total number of operations is <= 2e5, so every operation must be O(1) or amortized O(1). Values can make answers require 64-bit integers.
annotation.core_idea: Keep the current length n, total element sum S, and weighted sum W=sum a_i*i. Store elements in a deque and maintain a boolean reversed orientation. Append inserts at the logical back, which is deque back/front depending on orientation, and updates W += k*(n+1), S += k. Reversal maps every position i to n+1-i, so the new weighted sum is (n+1)*S - W; toggling the orientation flag avoids physically reversing. A cyclic shift moving the logical last element x to the logical front changes x's coefficient from n to 1 while every other element shifts one position right, so W becomes W - x*n + S; update the deque ends according to the orientation flag.
annotation.complexity: O(q) time and O(q) memory per test case
tricks: Maintain the weighted sum directly instead of rebuilding the array.
Use a direction flag so reverse only swaps the meaning of deque ends.
Compute the rotate contribution delta from the moved last element.

solution_variants.name: deque-with-orientation-flag
solution_variants.summary: Represent the logical array by a deque plus a reversed flag. Each operation updates the deque ends, total sum, length, and weighted sum using closed-form formulas.

tags.evidence: The implementation must support pushing and popping from both logical ends while the orientation may flip after reversals.
tags.description: Use a double-ended queue or equivalent structure where both ends are accessed or updated as part of the main algorithm.
tags.created_reason: The existing data-structure/queue tag does not capture algorithms that require symmetric access to both ends.

tags.evidence: The decisive idea is to maintain the weighted sum W and update it by formulas for append, reverse, and rotate.
The reversal update uses the identity W' = (n+1)*sum - W.
Careful handling of logical front/back under the reverse flag is the main source of implementation mistakes.
